transcript_quality: summary only — Granola meeting notes; Meeting 1 of 2
address: Hull city centre (ground floor flat with private terrace and parking; full address in entry-047)
lead_profile_text: Existing STL owner switching management company after 2 years with Residential Estates/Guests. Three main issues: (1) maintenance failures — toilet leak left unaddressed caused £1,600 damage wiping out entire month's earnings; fridge freezer breakdown during 3-month relocation booking; (2) no individual property identity — lumped into hotel-style listing with shared reviews and shared photos; (3) Wi-Fi problems — 4G mobile router, lost Amazon relocation client, negative reviews about connectivity. LSH managing fibre installation (currently delayed).
emotional_profile: Not a concept-validation lead — understands STL and is already earning from it. Switch decision entirely about service quality and trust. Does not need to be sold on STL; needs confidence Stayful won't repeat the same failures. Analytical.
delay_trigger_phrases: Isobel to decide over long weekend, respond next week
LSH fibre installation is the critical path item
delay_timeline: Decision over long weekend; respond next week. Wi-Fi fibre installation (LSH) must be resolved before go-live.
green_light_trigger_phrases: current income approximately £1,330/month average at 57% occupancy with current provider

top_objections.text: Current provider caused £1,600 damage through unaddressed toilet leak — how will Stayful prevent this?
top_objections.zac_response: Maintenance logging, batching and monthly reporting via Slack; no markup on contractors — transparent, proactive system

top_objections.text: Hotel-style listing with shared reviews and shared photos is harming her property's individual identity
top_objections.zac_response: Individual property listing confirmed as immediate fix — own listing, own reviews, own photos for Isobel's property only

top_objections.text: Wi-Fi problem (4G mobile router) led to negative reviews and a lost Amazon relocation client
top_objections.zac_response: Wi-Fi must be resolved before go-live; LSH fibre installation tracked as critical path item

top_objections.text: Concern about transition logistics — how to switch without disruption to existing bookings
top_objections.zac_response: Transition process: 1-month notice to current company, Stayful builds new listing from existing info, kickoff call to finalise

what_moved_them: Individual property listing (vs hotel-style shared listing) — explicit reaction; addresses biggest frustration directly
Maintenance logging, batching and monthly reporting via Slack — transparent system
No markup on contractors or cleaning (trust/transparency angle)
Property's unique strengths highlighted: terrace, parking, smart TV, better furnishing than competitors
Low competition in Hull = faster listing traction (avg 4.7 rating, 19 reviews per listing)

conversion_gaps.gap: Wi-Fi situation (4G router) — must be resolved before go-live; source of negative reviews and lost Amazon relocation client
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Track LSH fibre installation progress and confirm timeline before go-live. Do not proceed to live listing until Wi-Fi issue resolved.

offer_framing: 13%+VAT, expires 31 May 2026. No onboarding costs — property already furnished and photographed. Parking £63/month; potentially double-charged to guests.
str_net_monthly: £1,330 current (57% occupancy with Residential Estates/Guests); Stayful projection ~£2,417/month (£29k net annual at 71% occupancy)
